name: python3-impacket
title: python3-impacket
summary: Python3 module to easily build and dissect network protocols
description:
Python3 module to easily build and dissect network protocols
Impacket  is a  collection  of Python3  classes  focused on  providing
access  to network  packets.   Impacket allows  Python3 developers  to
craft and decode network packets in simple and consistent manner.  It
includes support for low-level protocols  such as IP, UDP and TCP, as
well as higher-level protocols such as NMB and SMB.
.
Impacket is highly  effective when used in conjunction  with a packet
capture utility or package such as Pcapy.  Packets can be constructed
from  scratch, as  well as  parsed from  raw data.   Furthermore, the
object  oriented API  makes  it  simple to  work  with deep  protocol
hierarchies.

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for impacket-netview.
arguments.raw: -netview  Option present in package usage line for impacket-netview.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use impacket-netview because Python3 module to easily build and dissect network protocols.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use impacket-netview as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `impacket-netview [options] [args]` as documented upstream for impacket-netview.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair impacket-netview with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `impacket-netview --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -netview
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for impacket-netview.
